summary: Joel briefly announces that the cover for his upcoming book has arrived from the publisher Apress. He humorously imagines future fame and jokes about the tedious copy-editing process where he has to revert changes back to his original writing. He also links to a story about poor customer service, noting that terrible service has become so normalized that egregious examples seem unremarkable.
key_points: Joel's book with Apress is nearing publication, with the cover design completed
The copy-editing process is frustrating, as Joel feels he needs to undo many of the editor's changes
Poor customer service has become so commonplace that horror stories no longer surprise anyone
money_quotes: Now I just have to go through about 10 chapters of copy edits and change everything back to the way I originally wrote it
What's so depressing about customer "service" these days is that when you read something like this, it sort of seems like par for the course.
key_insight: Poor customer service has become so normalized that even egregious failures are met with resignation rather than outrage.
